title: EPG Endpoint Learning and Deletion Churn
spl:
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:endpoint" earliest=-24h
| where action IN ("learn","delete","move")
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count as ops by _time, tenant, epg, action
| stats sum(eval(if(action=="learn",ops,0))) as learn_ops sum(eval(if(action=="delete",ops,0))) as del_ops max(_time) as last_window by tenant, epg
| where learn_ops>500 OR del_ops>500
| table tenant, epg, learn_ops, del_ops, last_window
| sort -learn_ops
description: Rapid endpoint learn/delete cycles on an EPG strain the control plane and can precede bridging or routing instability. Monitoring learning churn protects workload mobility designs and catches misconfigured duplicate IPs or spanning-tree interactions before they impact database and storage east-west paths.

Monitoring learning churn protects workload mobility designs and catches misconfigured duplicate IPs or spanning-tree interactions before they impact database and storage east-west paths.
grandmaExplanation: Rapid endpoint learn/delete cycles on an EPG strain the control plane and can precede bridging or routing instability ‚Äî so traffic problems, routing mistakes, and risky changes surface fast.
implementation: (1) Ingest endpoint tracker events at least every poll interval of APIC TA. (2) Baseline per business EPG; exclude known vMotion pools via lookup. (3) Correlate with faults on the same `dn` and with L3Out prefix churn (UC-18.1.14).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `TA_cisco-ACI`, APIC event log.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=cisco_aci` `sourcetype="cisco:aci:endpoint"` with fields `action`, `tenant`, `epg`, `mac`, `ip`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Ingest endpoint tracker events at least every poll interval of APIC TA. (2) Baseline per business EPG; exclude known vMotion pools via lookup. (3) Correlate with faults on the same `dn` and with L3Out prefix churn (UC-18.1.14).

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:endpoint" earliest=-24h
| where action IN ("learn","delete","move")
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count as ops by _time, tenant, epg, action
| stats sum(eval(if(action=="learn",ops,0))) as learn_ops sum(eval(if(action=="delete",ops,0))) as del_ops max(_time) as last_window by tenant, epg
| where learn_ops>500 OR del_ops>500
| table tenant, epg, learn_ops, del_ops, last_window
| sort -learn_ops
```

Understanding this SPL

**EPG Endpoint Learning and Deletion Churn** ‚Äî Rapid endpoint learn/delete cycles on an EPG strain the control plane and can precede bridging or routing instability. Monitoring learning churn protects workload mobility designs and catches misconfigured duplicate IPs or spanning-tree interactions before they impact database and storage east-west paths.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=cisco_aci` `sourcetype="cisco:aci:endpoint"` with fields `action`, `tenant`, `epg`, `mac`, `ip`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA_cisco-ACI`, APIC event log. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_aci; **sourcetype**: cisco:aci:endpoint. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=cisco_aci, sourcetype="cisco:aci:endpoint", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where action IN ("learn","delete","move")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Discretizes time or numeric ranges with `bin`/`bucket`.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time, tenant, epg, action** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by tenant, epg** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where learn_ops>500 OR del_ops>500` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ Pipeline stage (see **EPG Endpoint Learning and Deletion Churn**): table tenant, epg, learn_ops, del_ops, last_window
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Timechart (learn vs delete), Table (noisy EPGs), Single value (EPGs over threshold).
visualization: Timechart (learn vs delete), Table (noisy EPGs), Single value (EPGs over threshold).
